always_blocks.text_preview:
always @(*)
    begin
      rdata = 64'b0; 
      if (en) begin
        case(address[8:3])
            0:
                rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[0] ? 'b0 : {31'b0, start};
        endcase
      end // if

asset_candidates.name: jtag_hash
asset_candidates.description: 256-bit debug authentication token/hash, candidate protected secret.

asset_candidates.name: jtag_unlock
asset_candidates.description: Debug unlock state signal, indicates whether JTAG authentication has succeeded.

asset_candidates.name: reglk_ctrl
asset_candidates.description: Register lock control bits for peripherals; controls write protection.

asset_candidates.name: acc_ctrl
asset_candidates.description: Access control configuration bits, e.g., privilege-based access to boot ROM.

asset_candidates.name: bootrom
asset_candidates.description: Boot ROM memory, candidate protected code/configuration store.

asset_candidates.name: fuse_values
asset_candidates.description: Fuse read path providing security configuration or key material.

asset_candidates.name: crypto_data
asset_candidates.description: Data interfaces to AES, SHA, HMAC engines; may carry plaintext/keys.

asset_candidates.name: reset_controls
asset_candidates.description: Software-triggered reset outputs; can affect security state.

subject_candidates.name: JTAG_debug_interface
subject_candidates.description: External JTAG debugger connected to the chip; can issue DTM/DMI transactions.

subject_candidates.name: SimDTM
subject_candidates.description: Debug transport module providing debug access to the system bus.

subject_candidates.name: CPU_privilege_level
subject_candidates.description: Privilege level of the executing hart; influences access control decisions.

subject_candidates.name: debug_mode
subject_candidates.description: Indicates whether the core is in debug mode; may relax access checks.

subject_candidates.name: testmode
subject_candidates.description: Test mode input; may enable alternative access paths.

subject_candidates.name: AXI_requester
subject_candidates.description: Any bus master that can issue AXI transactions to peripherals, e.g., core LSU, DMA engine.

operation_candidates.name: unlock_jtag
operation_candidates.description: Assert jtag_unlock after successful debug authentication.

operation_candidates.name: reset_assertion
operation_candidates.description: Assert a software-triggered reset line to a peripheral or subsystem.

operation_candidates.name: configure_access_control
operation_candidates.description: Write to acc_ctrl or reglk_ctrl configuration registers.

guard_candidates.name: privilege_level_check
guard_candidates.description: Access to boot ROM is gated by (rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]).

guard_candidates.name: register_lock_mediation
guard_candidates.description: reglk_ctrl bits prevent writes to protected peripheral registers.

guard_candidates.name: debug_unlock_bypass
guard_candidates.description: jtag_unlock signal allows bypass of register locks in reglk_wrapper.

guard_candidates.name: write_enable_flags
guard_candidates.description: we_flag inputs indicate permitted write operations from upstream PMP/decoder.

guard_candidates.name: debug_mode_gate
guard_candidates.description: debug_mode_i may disable certain protections or enable debug-only paths.

state_lifecycle_facts.reset_value: 0 (locked), set after authentication, unclear clear condition, possibly only reset.
determined by boot/fuse? Can be set by SW, cleared only by reset or jtag_unlock bypass.
unknown, but used with priv_lvl_i to gate access.

state_lifecycle_facts.description: Read-only path; fuse content is static after manufacturing. No write/clear condition.

path_candidates.description: JTAG -> dmi_jtag -> debug_req -> dm_slave -> dm_master, allowing debugger to issue bus transactions.
path_candidates.concern: May bypass CPU privilege checks and access control.

path_candidates.description: Bus request -> rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0] -> boot ROM read.
path_candidates.concern: If acc_ctrl is misconfigured or bypassed, unprivileged may read boot ROM.

path_candidates.description: AXI write -> acct_wrapper -> reglk_wrapper -> peripheral, subject to acc_ctrl and reglk_ctrl.
path_candidates.concern: Weak lock or bypass (via jtag_unlock) could allow unauthorized register modification.

path_candidates.description: AXI write -> rst_wrapper -> rst_X outputs, can reset peripherals.
path_candidates.concern: Unprivileged SW could trigger resets of security-critical modules.

path_candidates.description: jtag_unlock signal from debug logic feeds directly into reglk_wrapper, bypassing register locks.
path_candidates.concern: Once debug is authenticated, all register locks are bypassed regardless of privilege.

summary: Labeled permission-oriented facts from the given RTL scope: identified debug/JTAG paths that may bypass access controls, register lock mechanisms, privilege-based boot ROM gate, fuse read path, and software-triggered reset controls. Several guard candidates (privilege check, register lock, debug unlock) and state lifecycle assumptions are noted for further analysis.
